title: US easing ban on Iranian oil would signal ‘beginning of the end of war’

title: Will Russian oil be the biggest winner in the US-Israel war on Iran?
description: The price of Russian Urals has soared in recent weeks. How Russia - and other energy producing nations - could profit.

title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine wants timeline for next round of Russia talks
description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says clear dates needed as Ukrainian negotiators prepare for discussions in US.

title: Israel strikes Syria after Druze clashes
description: Syria condemned the strikes on weapons sites and government infrastructure as an "outrageous" assault on Syria's sovereignty.

title: BTS Leans Into Korean History With ‘Arirang.’ Here Are the Major References.
description: The group’s new album “Arirang” includes a nod to a beloved Korean folk song and other important historical references.

title: Iran War Underscores the Diverging Aims of Trump and Netanyahu
description: The United States views Iran through a prism of global responsibilities and strategic goals. Israel has a more regional approach. After nearly three weeks of war, their paths are diverging.

title: Чак Норрис умер в возрасте 86 лет
description: Легендарный американский киноактер Чак Норрис умер в возрасте 86 лет. Об этом сообщает The New York Post со ссылкой на заявление семьи.

title: Haifa refinery says more missile attack damage found near site, but most production online
description:
Facility will take 'few days' to restore; Haifa's deputy mayor urges government to close refineries as soon as possible: 'Playing Russian roulette with residents' lives'
The post Haifa refinery says more missile attack damage found near site, but most production online appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Joe Kent hints to Tucker Carlson that Israel may have killed Charlie Kirk to stoke Iran war
description:
Days after resigning and under investigation for sharing classified information, the former counterterrorism head adds to the growing debate over Israel and antisemitism on the right
The post Joe Kent hints to Tucker Carlson that Israel may have killed Charlie Kirk to stoke Iran war appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Despite IDF strikes, Iran’s feared Basij security forces still patrol Tehran’s streets
description:
Regime-loyal volunteers for highly decentralized force man dozens of checkpoints; over 100 Iranians arrested for alleged ties to enemy states, some for holding Starlink internet dishes
The post Despite IDF strikes, Irans feared Basij security forces still patrol Tehrans streets appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Hegseth on seeking $200 billion for Iran war: "That number could move"
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed the Pentagon would ask Congress for more money to wage war in Iran, though he said the reported $200 billion figure "could move."Why it matters: A request on that scale would likely face stiff opposition from many Democrats and some Republicans, given the unpopularity of the war and the fact that it would be in addition to the Pentagon's existing $1 trillion budget.Driving the news: The Washington Post rep

title: Iranian missile attacks damage Rehovot homes; IDF reveals it killed Basij intel chief
description:
Two missiles fired by Iran had cluster warheads, causing mostly light injuries; IRGC spokesman denies PM's claim on destroyed missile production capacity, is killed by the time he gets quoted
The post Iranian missile attacks damage Rehovot homes; IDF reveals it killed Basij intel chief appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: IDF hits Iranian Navy vessels, command centres in Caspian Sea
description:
The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) has announced that it struck Iranian naval infrastructure in the Caspian Sea.
The post IDF hits Iranian Navy vessels, command centres in Caspian Sea appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: Oil prices, fear of Trump? China mysteriously reduced warplane activity near Taiwan
description: Analysts are puzzled by a recent decrease in Chinese warplane sorties toward Taiwan.

title: A-10 Warthogs target Iranian fast-attack craft in Strait of Hormuz
description: The A-10’s deployment in Operation Epic Fury comes as Congress has moved to protect it from Air Force divestment efforts.

title: US carrier Ford to go to port temporarily after fire
description: The USS Gerald R. Ford, deployed in operations against Iran, is expected to temporarily go to Souda Bay on the Greek island of Crete, officials said.

title: Number of US troops wounded in war against Iran rises to about 200
description: The U.S. military’s Central Command said the vast majority of those wounded had suffered minor injuries and 180 troops had already returned to duty.

title: Ukraine opens battlefield AI data to allies in world-first move
description: Ukraine is giving international partners and defense companies access to its trove of real combat data to train AI models for autonomous drone systems.

title: Iran’s new supreme leader was ‘wounded and likely disfigured’ in US-Israeli strikes, Hegseth contends
description: Khamenei has remained out of view since his selection on Sunday by Iran’s clerical establishment.

title: US Air Force KC-135 goes down in Iraq, CENTCOM says
description: “The incident was not due to hostile fire or friendly fire,” the release said, adding that rescue efforts are ongoing.

title: EU eyes energy tax cuts to counter impact of Iran war
description: The war in the Middle East is having an increasingly significant impact on energy markets. In recent days, strikes by both Iran and Israel have caused extensive damage to the region’s energy infrastructure, and prices are skyrocketing. Meeting in Brussels for a European summit, European leaders discussed measures to be implemented to curb the surge in prices, including lifting caps on state aid for energy-intensive industrial sectors and reducing

title: Spokesperson for Iran’s Revolutionary Guard killed in strike
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Friday that US-Israeli strikes had killed their spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini. It came shortly after Naini had insisted that Tehran was still building missiles, seeking to counter a claim by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu that it no longer could.

title: French billionaire Bolloré to face corruption trial linked to African elections
description: French ⁠billionaire Vincent Bolloré will stand trial on charges of embezzlement and corruption related to election campaigns in two African countries. Bolloré is ⁠suspected of having bribed foreign officials ‌during the election campaign of presidents Faure Gnassingbe ⁠in Togo and Alpha Conde in Guinea, prosecutors said.

title: French navy seizes oil tanker sailing from Russia accused of flying false flag in Mediterranean
description: The French navy has boarded and seized an oil tanker sailing in the Western ‌Mediterranean from the Russian port of Murmansk accused of belonging to what has been dubbed Moscow's "shadow fleet", vessels of opaque ownership suspected of dodging Western sanctions on the country's crude oil exports.

title: The West Bank, up against the wall: Illegal Palestinian workers face exploitation and danger
description: Our reporters in the Middle East went to meet Palestinians who are desperately trying to reach Israel to find work there. Before the October 7 attacks, 120,000 Palestinian workers held permits allowing them to enter Israel legally. Today, only a handful of permits are issued. Facing an economic crisis devastating the occupied West Bank, many have no choice but to take the illegal route.
